title: Customer experience outweighs brand in AI-assisted shopping
description:
AI recommendation engines rely on reviews, comparisons, and customer signals to decide which brands to surface and trust.
The post Customer experience outweighs brand in AI-assisted shopping appeared first on MarTech.

title: Who owns agentic workflows? Agencies struggle to govern new tools as marketing budgets surge
description: Deciding how AI is used, vetting tools, shaping best practices and how staff are incentivized to use AI tools are still up for debate internally at agencies.

title: Target taps former Walmart exec as supply chain, logistics chief
description: Jeff England, who worked to improve inventory availability at QXO, will join the retailer on May 31 as it seeks to strengthen in-stock reliability.

title: At Home CEO to retire after leading it through bankruptcy
description: Chief Executive Officer Brad Weston will transition to an advisory role and the home retailer has initiated a search for his replacement.

title: Home Depot addresses cost changes surrounding tariffs, oil prices
description:
Richard McPhail, chief financial officer at Home Depot, has reaffirmed the retailer’s sales guidance in light of multiple rising costs, including fuel prices, interest rates and tariffs. Home Depot expects comparable sales growth to be in a range from flat to 2% growth. That follows a quarter in which Home Depot comparable sales increased 0.6% […]
The post Home Depot addresses cost changes surrounding tariffs, oil prices appeared first on Digital Commerce 360.

title: Google’s new Universal Cart wants to follow your entire shopping journey across the internet
description: Most people shop across multiple devices, many retailers, and over the course of many days, which is why Google is launching Universal Cart.

title: Google Revamps Its Iconic Search Bar for the First Time in 25 Years
description: Google readies its Search bar for the AI era, answering longer queries, and simplifying shopping across the internet.

title: Microsoft Ad Chief Kya Sainsbury-Carter Departs; LinkedIn’s Matt Derella Takes Over
description: Microsoft’s top advertising executive, Kya Sainsbury-Carter, is leaving the company after three years in the role, to be replaced by LinkedIn ads exec Matt Derella.

title: Risks to look out for when using vibe coding to replace SaaS
description:
Vibe coding can replace SaaS tools fast, but risks in security, integration and maintenance can quickly outweigh the savings.
The post Risks to look out for when using vibe coding to replace SaaS appeared first on MarTech.

title: GA4 now tracks AI chatbot traffic automatically
description:
Google Analytics just made it easier to see how much traffic AI assistants are sending to your website.
The post GA4 now tracks AI chatbot traffic automatically appeared first on MarTech.

title: Advancing content provenance for a safer, more transparent AI ecosystem
description: OpenAI advances AI content provenance with Content Credentials, SynthID, and a verification tool to help people identify and trust AI-generated media.

title: Amazon Business rolls out new credit cards with U.S. Bank and Mastercard
description:
Amazon.com Inc. is updating its business credit card program, shifting new applicants to U.S. Bank and Mastercard as it adds more rewards and financing tools for business buyers. The retailer introduced the new Prime Business Card and Amazon Business Card on May 13. The launch moves new applicants away from American Express, which had issued […]
The post Amazon Business rolls out new credit cards with U.S. Bank and Mastercard appeared first on Digital Commerce 360.

title: Parts Town updates PartPredictor with new AI-backed features
description:
Parts Town has upgraded its AI-powered PartPredictor tool with expanded coverage of 120 brands featuring over 18,000 equipment models. The expansion will allow technicians and dispatchers to identify the correct parts faster using everything from model numbers to plain-language symptom descriptions. The distributor — which handles original equipment manufacturer (OEM) parts for food service; heating, […]
The post Parts Town updates PartPredictor with new AI-backed features appeared first on Digital Commerce 360.

title: #213: AI Answers - What AI Should Never Do, Enterprise Scaling, Governing AI & Navigating IT Roadblocks
description:
Leadership wants AI everywhere. IT security can't keep up. Marketing is racing ahead while legal and finance dig in. And every week brings another story of an AI agent doing something nobody told it to do. Paul Roetzer and Cathy McPhillips answer 15 listener questions on how to actually move organizations forward and where the real opportunities lie for individuals, SMBs, and enterprises right now.

00:00:00 — Intro

00:07:01 — How do you move a company out of AI policy paralysis?

00:08:54 — How should regulated, hands-on teams introduce AI?

00:12:30 — When companies are stuck, what tends to get them moving?

00:15:00 — Should IT security evolve to adopt AI quicker or should businesses slow down?

00:17:43 — What changes an AI-skeptic employee's mind?

00:21:18 — How should early-career professionals prioritize what to learn?

00:23:29 — Is there a point where you should stop learning and start building?

00:27:29 — Where do companies get stuck scaling AI across departments?

00:29:4

title: U.S. CPG manufacturers are sitting on excess capacity, which could be a boon for brands
description: Keychain's, CPG Intelligence Report showed that one major theme companies are grappling with is significant overcapacity.
